title: 8 Heart Checks Before You Lose Hope
hook: Prayers bouncing off the ceiling? Run these 8 heart checks before you lose hope.
script: Prayers bouncing off the ceiling? Run these 8 heart checks before you lose hope. One, motives—James 4:3 asks why we ask. Two, unforgiveness—Mark 11:25 says forgive when you pray. Three, persistence—Luke 18 shows God honors steady knocking. Four, timing—Ecclesiastes 3 says there’s a season. Five, obedience—1 John 3:22 links request and response. Six, agreement—Matthew 18:19 invites united faith. Seven, faith over doubt—James 1 warns double‑mindedness. Eight, warfare—Daniel 10 shows delays, not denials. Take these to God today. He isn’t rejecting you; He’s refining you. Pray, Search me, O God, and lead me, Psalm 139:23–24. Save this to revisit on hard days.

title: When Heaven Feels Quiet
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Silence isn’t absence; sometimes it’s a heart check. Start here: motives—James 4:3, am I asking to know Him or just get a thing? Unforgiveness—Mark 11:25, who do I need to release? Persistence—Luke 18, have I stopped knocking? Timing—Ecclesiastes 3, could the season be preparing me? Obedience—1 John 3:22, is there a step I’m avoiding? Agreement—Matthew 18:19, have I asked others to pray? Faith—James 1, am I doubting God’s character? Warfare—Daniel 10, could there be unseen resistance? Whisper, Lord, align my heart and strengthen my hope. He hears you. Don’t quit.

title: Delayed, Not Denied: 8 Checks in 4 Pairs
hook: You need to hear this today: your prayer isn’t unanswered—it’s under review.
script: You need to hear this today: your prayer isn’t unanswered—it’s under review. Do four quick pairs of heart checks from Scripture. Motive and obedience: James 4:3 and 1 John 3:22. Unforgiveness and agreement: Mark 11:25 and Matthew 18:19. Persistence and timing: Luke 18 and Ecclesiastes 3. Faith and warfare: James 1 and Daniel 10. Psychology agrees—expectancy can make silence feel like no, and perseverance grows hope. God’s delays are not God’s disinterest. Pray, Father, align my desires, cleanse my heart, and strengthen my faith as I wait. Save this and revisit the pairs this week. Your hope is not wasted.
